ertations Research Support Frankie Vasquez fvasquez@berkeley.edu Biography Sergey Levine received a BS and MS in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2009, and a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2014. He joined the facult

Stanford University in 2014. He joined the faculty of the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley in fall 2016. His work focuses on machine learning for decision making and control, with an emphasis on deep learning

ing and control, with an emphasis on deep learning and reinforcement learning algorithms. Applications of his work include autonomous robots and vehicles, as well as computer vision and graphics. His research includes developing algorithms for end-to

research includes developing algorithms for end-to-end training of deep neural network policies that combine perception and control, scalable algorithms for inverse reinforcement learning, deep reinforcement learning algorithms, and more. Education 2

rcement learning algorithms, and more. Education 2014, Ph.D., Computer Science, Stanford University 2009, B.S/M.S., Computer Science, Stanford University Research Areas Artificial Intelligence (AI) Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR) Res
